title: Role Based Access Control Good Practices — Access toproxysubresource of Nodes
text:
Users with access to the
nodes/proxy
sub-resource have rights to the Kubelet API,
which allows for command execution on every pod on the node(s) to which they have rights.
This access bypasses audit logging and admission control, so care should be taken before
granting any rights to this resource.
These APIs can be exercised via websocket HTTP
GET
requests, which only requires authorization of the
get
verb.
This means that
get
permission on
nodes/proxy
is not a read-only permission.
For example, permission to
get
nodes/proxy
provides access to privileged kubelet
APIs that can retrieve container logs or execute and attach to pod processes,
even when a caller does not have the equivalent permissions through the
Kubernetes API.
See
Kubelet authentication/authorization
for more information.
